flashy.models.tree package

Submodules

flashy.models.tree.constructor module

flashy.models.tree.constructor.create_root(root_name)

Create a root node for a configuration tree.

Parameters:

root_name (str) – Name of the root node.

Returns:

Newly created root TreeNode.

Return type:

TreeNode

flashy.models.tree.constructor.find_by_display_name(name, mapping_values)

Find an item in a mapping list by its display name.

Parameters:

  • name (str) – Display name to search for.

  • mapping_values – Iterable of objects containing a display_name attribute.

Returns:

Matching item or None if not found.

flashy.models.tree.constructor.convert_config(tag, values, config_cls)

Convert a raw dictionary into a typed configuration object.

This function wraps msgspec.convert() to attach a tag and convert values into the target configuration class.

Parameters:

  • tag – Tag used for polymorphic decoding.

  • values – Dictionary of configuration values.

  • config_cls – Target configuration class type.

Returns:

Instantiated configuration object.

flashy.models.tree.constructor.ensure_path(parent_node, path)

Ensure that a hierarchical path exists in a tree. Missing intermediate group nodes are created automatically.

Parameters:

  • parent_node (TreeNode) – Starting node of the traversal.

  • path (list[str]) – Sequence of group names defining the path.

Returns:

Final node corresponding to the last path segment.

Return type:

TreeNode

flashy.models.tree.constructor.add_container_parameters(parent_node, container)

Add all parameters from a container into a tree structure. Each parameter is inserted under its defined hierarchical path.

Parameters:

  • parent_node (TreeNode) – Root node to attach parameters to.

  • container – Parameter container exposing DEFINITIONS.

flashy.models.tree.constructor.get_branch_values(node)

Recursively collect parameter values from a tree branch.

Parameters:

node (TreeNode) – Root node of the branch.

Returns:

Dictionary mapping parameter keys to values.

Return type:

dict[str, Any]

flashy.models.tree.constructor.combine_root_trees(trees, root_name='root')

Combine multiple trees under a single root node.

Parameters:

  • trees (list[TreeNode]) – List of root trees to combine.

  • root_name (str) – Name of the new combined root.

Returns:

New root node containing all input trees.

Return type:

TreeNode

flashy.models.tree.constructor.construct_user_tree(config, root_name='root')

Construct a tree representation of the user configuration.

Parameters:

  • config (UserConfig) – User configuration container.

  • root_name (str) – Name of the root node.

Returns:

Root of the constructed tree.

Return type:

TreeNode

flashy.models.tree.constructor.construct_analysis_tree(config, root_name='root')

Construct a tree representation of the analysis configuration.

Parameters:

  • config (AnalysisConfig) – Analysis configuration container.

  • root_name (str) – Name of the root node.

Returns:

Root of the constructed tree.

Return type:

TreeNode

flashy.models.tree.constructor.construct_digitizers_trees(config, root_name='root')

Construct a hierarchical tree of digitizer configurations.

Each digitizer is grouped under its own container node, and each channel is represented as a subgroup containing parameter nodes.

Parameters:

  • config (DigitizersConfig) – Digitizer configuration container.

  • root_name (str) – Name of the root node.

Returns:

Root node of the digitizer tree.

Return type:

TreeNode

flashy.models.tree.constructor.construct_detectors_trees(config, root_name='root')

Construct a tree representation of detector configurations.

Parameters:

  • config (DetectorsConfig) – Detector configuration container.

  • root_name (str) – Name of the root node.

Returns:

Root node of the detector tree.

Return type:

TreeNode

flashy.models.tree.constructor.build_analysis_config(root_node)

Extract analysis configuration values from a tree.

Parameters:

root_node (TreeNode) – Root node of the analysis tree.

Returns:

Dictionary of analysis parameters.

Return type:

dict[str, Any]

flashy.models.tree.constructor.build_digitizer_config(root_node)

Build a digitizer configuration object from a tree.

Parameters:

root_node (TreeNode) – Root node of the digitizer tree.

Returns:

Typed digitizer configuration.

Return type:

Digitizer

Raises:

NotImplementedError – If digitizer type is not registered.

flashy.models.tree.constructor.build_detectors_config(root_node)

Build detector configuration objects from a tree.

Parameters:

root_node (TreeNode) – Root node of the detector tree.

Returns:

List of detector configuration objects.

Return type:

list[Detector]

flashy.models.tree.delegate module

class flashy.models.tree.delegate.ParameterDelegate(parent=None)

Bases: QStyledItemDelegate

Qt item delegate responsible for editing parameter values in a view.

This delegate customizes the editing widgets used for parameter nodes in a ParameterTreeModel. Depending on the parameter definition, it dynamically creates appropriate editors.It also populates editors with the current parameter values and ensures proper synchronization between the view and the underlying model.

Inherits:

PySide6.QtWidgets.QStyledItemDelegate

createEditor(parent, option, index)

Create an appropriate editor widget for a parameter.

The type of editor is selected based on the parameter’s widget type defined in its ParameterDefinition:

  • combobox → QComboBox

  • entry with parser → QLineEdit

  • entry with float → QDoubleSpinBox

  • entry with int → QSpinBox

  • otherwise → default editor

Parameters:

  • parent (QWidget) – Parent widget for the editor.

  • option (QStyleOptionViewItem) – Style options for item rendering.

  • index (QModelIndex) – Model index being edited.

Returns:

Editor widget instance.

Return type:

QWidget

setEditorData(editor, index)

Populate the editor widget with the current model value by extracting the value from the underlying TreeNode and initializes the appropriate editor widget.

Parameters:

  • editor (QWidget) – Editor widget created by createEditor().

  • index (QModelIndex) – Model index being edited.

setModelData(editor, model, index)
updateEditorGeometry(editor, option, index)

flashy.models.tree.model module

class flashy.models.tree.model.ParameterTreeModel(root_node)

Bases: QAbstractItemModel

Qt model exposing a hierarchical parameter tree to Qt item views.

This model adapts a TreeNode hierarchy into a Qt-compatible item model, allowing parameter groups, containers, and leaf parameters to be displayed and edited in views such as QTreeView.

It supports:

  • Hierarchical navigation (parent/child relationships)

  • Editable parameter values

  • Checkbox, editable, and read-only widgets

  • Tooltips for parameter descriptions

Inherits:

PySide6.QtCore.QAbstractItemModel

rowCount(parent=<PySide6.QtCore.QModelIndex(-1, -1, 0x0, QObject(0x0))>)
Return type:

int

columnCount(parent=<PySide6.QtCore.QModelIndex(-1, -1, 0x0, QObject(0x0))>)
Return type:

int

index(row, column, /, parent=<PySide6.QtCore.QModelIndex(-1, -1, 0x0, QObject(0x0))>)

Create an index for a given row, column, and parent.

Parameters:

  • row (int) – Row index within parent.

  • column (int) – Column index.

  • parent (QModelIndex | QPersistentModelIndex) – Parent index.

Returns:

Qt model index representing the node.

Return type:

QModelIndex

parent(child)

Return the parent index of a given child index.

Parameters:

child (QModelIndex | QPersistentModelIndex) – Child index.

Returns:

Parent index or invalid index if root.

Return type:

QModelIndex

flags(index)

Return item flags for a given index.

Parameters:

index (QModelIndex | QPersistentModelIndex) – Model index.

Returns:

Qt item flags.

Return type:

Qt.ItemFlag

headerData(section, orientation, /, role)

Return header labels for the model.

Parameters:

  • section (int) – Column index.

  • orientation (Qt.Orientation) – Header orientation.

  • role (int) – Data role.

Returns:

Header label or None.

Return type:

Any

data(index, /, role)

Return data for a given index and role.

Parameters:

  • index (QModelIndex) – Model index.

  • role (int) – Data role requested by the view.

Returns:

Role-dependent data or None.

Return type:

Any

setData(index, value, /, role)

Set the value of a parameter in the model.

Updates the underlying TreeNode value and emits change notifications to update the Qt view.

Parameters:

  • index (QModelIndex) – Model index.

  • value (Any) – New value to assign.

  • role (int) – Data role triggering the update.

Returns:

True if the value was successfully updated, False otherwise.

Return type:

bool

flashy.models.tree.node module

class flashy.models.tree.node.TreeNode(name, *, node_type='group', definition=None, container=None, parent=None)

Bases: object

Node of a hierarchical configuration tree.

This class represents a generic tree structure used to organize parameter definitions and configuration containers in a hierarchical manner. Nodes can represent groups, parameters, containers, or the root of the tree.

Each node may optionally hold a ParameterDefinition and/or a ParameterContainer, allowing it to act as both a structural and data-binding element within configuration trees.

Variables:

  • name (str) – Internal name of the node.

  • node_type (Literal["root", "container", "group", "parameter"]) – Type of node (“root”, “container”, “group”, “parameter”).

  • definition (ParameterDefinition | None) – Optional parameter definition associated with the node.

  • container (ParameterContainer | None) – Optional parameter container associated with the node.

  • parent (TreeNode | None) – Parent node in the tree, if any.

  • children (list[TreeNode]) – List of child nodes.

add_child(child)

Add a child node to this node. The child’s parent reference is updated automatically.

Parameters:

child (TreeNode) – Node to add as a child.

remove_child(child)

Remove a child node from this node. If the child exists, it is detached and its parent reference is cleared.

Parameters:

child (TreeNode) – Node to remove.

child(row)

Return the child node at a given index.

Parameters:

row (int) – Index of the child.

Returns:

Child node or None if index is invalid.

Return type:

TreeNode | None

child_count()

Return the number of children of this node.

Returns:

Number of child nodes.

Return type:

int

row()

Return this node’s index within its parent’s children list.

Returns:

Index position in parent, or 0 if root.

Return type:

int

find_path(*path)

Traverse the tree following a sequence of display names. Each path element is matched against display_name().

Parameters:

path (str) – Sequence of node display names.

Returns:

Target node if found, otherwise None.

Return type:

TreeNode | None

display_name()

Return the display name of the node.

If a ParameterDefinition is attached, its name is used; otherwise the internal node name is returned.

Returns:

Display name of the node.

Return type:

str

description()

Return the node description if a definition is attached.

Returns:

Node description or empty string.

Return type:

str

get_value()

Retrieve the value associated with this node. Only applies if the node is bound to a container and definition.

Returns:

Stored parameter value or None.

set_value(value)

Set the value associated with this node. Only applies if the node is bound to a container and definition.

Parameters:

value – New value to assign.

property is_root

Whether this node is the root of the tree.

property is_container

Whether this node represents a container.

property is_group

Whether this node represents a group.

property is_parameter

Whether this node represents a parameter leaf.
